title: Freedom Hits the Front Page
story: Extra, extra! The Pennsylvania Evening Gazette made history in 1776. On July 6, its front page boldly announced the American Declaration of Independence. Imagine the excitement as people crowded around to read about their new nation. This newspaper spread the word of freedom far and wide, helping spark the American Revolution.
activity_question: What did the Pennsylvania Evening Gazette announce on its front page?
follow_up_question: Why do you think newspapers were important for spreading news in 1776?

title: Brush Strokes of Brilliance
story: A paintbrush came to life in the hands of a newborn boy. Oswaldo Guayasamín, destined to become Ecuador's most famous artist, entered the world on July 6, 1919. Born in Quito, he grew up to create powerful paintings and sculptures that captured the spirit of Latin America. His art spoke volumes about human struggles and triumphs.
activity_question: Where was Oswaldo Guayasamín born?
bonus_fact: Guayasamín's art often featured expressive hands, which he saw as symbols of human emotion and work.

title: Bessie Head: A Voice from Botswana
story: Bessie Head, a powerful storyteller, entered the world in 1937. Born in South Africa, she later made Botswana her home. Head's books, like 'When Rain Clouds Gather' and 'Maru', painted vivid pictures of African life. Her words touched hearts across the globe, sharing tales of struggle and hope. Though she left us in 1986, her stories continue to inspire readers on July 6 and beyond.
activity_question: Which country did Bessie Head call home later in life?
follow_up_question: Can you name one of Bessie Head's books mentioned in the story?

title: A Voice That Made It Rain Men
story: Izora Rhodes Armstead belted out tunes that made people dance! Born in Houston, Texas on July 6, 1942, she grew up to become a disco sensation. Armstead was part of the group Two Tons O' Fun, which later became The Weather Girls. Their hit song 'It's Raining Men' had everyone singing along and moving to the beat.
activity_question: What famous song did Izora Rhodes Armstead perform with The Weather Girls?
follow_up_question: Can you think of other songs about weather?

title: Cricket Star Born in South Africa
story: Makhaya Ntini burst onto the cricket scene, swinging balls and taking wickets. Born in 1977 in Mdingi, Cape Province, this South African cricketer would go on to become a national hero. On July 6, the same date years later, fans celebrated the birthday of this fast bowler who inspired a generation of young athletes.
activity_question: Where was Makhaya Ntini born?
follow_up_question: What position did Ntini play in cricket?
